title: Tennessee Fetal Heartbeat Bill—A Step in the Wrong Direction
author: Erika Schanzenbach
orientation: Erika Schanzenbach argues that Tennessee's SB2196 fetal heartbeat bill is not a step in the right direction because it will be enjoined by the courts, regulates abortion as healthcare instead of criminalizing it as murder, leaves enforcement to abortionists, and shields mothers from prosecution. She frames the abolitionist alternative as biblical justice that obeys God's clear commands rather than submitting to wicked SCOTUS opinions.
audiences: Pro-life Christian celebrating the heartbeat bill
Pastor teaching biblical justice and civil magistrates
Skeptical secular reader asking why bans don't end abortion

questions.question: Why does the author refuse to celebrate Tennessee's SB2196 even though pro-life friends call it a step in the right direction?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian celebrating the heartbeat bill
questions.answer: Because evaluating direction requires markers—our current location, a destination, and a map—and by those measures the bill moves away from biblical justice rather than toward it. It will not actually save a single baby and submits to the very court opinions it claims to oppose.
questions.quote: All in all, this bill is not going to save one single baby. It will be blocked by the courts before it ever goes into force.

questions.question: What three things does the author say we need in order to know whether our steps are going in the right direction?
questions.answer: We need an honest assessment of where we are, a clearly defined goal, and a reliable guide for getting there. Without all three, claims of progress are arbitrary.
questions.quote:
If we want to know if our steps are going in the right direction, we need:

1. an understanding of our current location
2. a specific destination to aim for
3. a map so we don’t get lost

questions.question: How does the author describe the current state of abortion in America after 47 years of pro-life activism?
questions.audience: Skeptical secular reader asking why bans don't end abortion
questions.answer: She describes a normalized, legalized holocaust in which roughly one in four women have an abortion by age 45 and hundreds of thousands of babies are killed every year, with chemical abortions increasingly going uncounted.
questions.quote: In the fight against abortion our current location is smack dab in the middle of a legalized holocaust. The shedding of innocent blood is so common that according to [Guttmacher Institute](https://www.guttmacher.org/fact-sheet/induced-abortion-united-states), approximately one in four women will have an abortion by age 45.

questions.question: What does the author identify as the proper destination of legislation against abortion?
questions.audience: Pastor teaching biblical justice and civil magistrates
questions.answer: Justice—not merely cultural change or saving some babies along the way, but establishing justice for victims and restraining evil, because that is what God commands of civil magistrates.
questions.quote:
Because law isn’t about erasing all evil from society, it is about establishing justice for victims and restraining evil as much as is possible.

*Justice*, that’s our destination.

questions.question: What biblical case law does the author cite to argue God demands justice for the child in the womb?
questions.audience: Pastor teaching biblical justice and civil magistrates
questions.answer: Exodus 21:22-23, which she reads as case law requiring eye-for-eye, life-for-life justice when a preborn child is harmed, demonstrating that the image-bearing child in the womb is owed equal protection.
questions.quote: On top of these crystal clear instructions, He gave us case law in Exodus 21:22-23 explaining that if someone strikes a woman and the child she is carrying is delivered early and harmed in any way, the one who struck her must pay eye for eye, tooth for tooth, life for life. We can deduce from this that God demands justice for the child in the womb, the child He fearfully and wonderfully made in His own image.

questions.question: Why does leaving heartbeat detection to abortionists undermine SB2196's pragmatic effectiveness?
questions.answer: Because ultrasound at six weeks is operator dependent and the staff with financial incentive to abort are the same people deciding whether a heartbeat is found, with no enforcement mechanism beyond a self-reported note in the medical record.
questions.quote: **The abortionists are left on the honor system**. All this bill requires is that the abortionist note in the woman’s medical record that an ultrasound was done and no heartbeat was detected. No one is checking up on this. There is no enforcement mechanism.

questions.question: How does SB2196's narrow ban on sex, race, and Down syndrome abortions actually fail in practice?
questions.answer: It permits abortion for any other reason or no reason at all, and since abortionists are not required to ask why a woman is aborting, women who want to abort for the prohibited reasons will simply lie—rendering the carve-out unenforceable.
questions.quote: SB2196 allows women to abort for literally any reason besides race, sex, or Downs syndrome diagnosis, or for no reason at all. Women are not stupid. If they want to abort their children for any of these *off-limits* reasons, they will lie about it.

questions.question: What is the central justice problem the author identifies with treating abortion as healthcare rather than murder?
questions.answer: Categorizing abortion as healthcare turns the bill into a regulation of how killing may be done rather than a criminalization of the killing itself, fundamentally misnaming the act.
questions.quote: Throughout this bill categorizes abortion as healthcare, regulates it like healthcare, discusses the health effects on women and the state’s interest in whether or not the fetus feels pain during the procedure. This is a regulatory bill on how something may be done, not the criminalization of a murderous practice.

questions.question: Why does the author say Tennessee lawmakers are complicit in evil rather than resisting it?
questions.answer: Because they admit preborn children are human beings from conception yet voluntarily submit to SCOTUS opinions that require them to permit those children's killing, instead of nullifying wicked court rulings as their constitutional duty demands.
questions.quote: Instead of defying and nullifying wicked and unconstitutional court opinions, they choose to submit to and enforce them, making them active participants in the evil.

questions.question: On what grounds does the author argue that mothers seeking abortion should not be exempted from prosecution?
questions.answer: She argues that a woman who deliberately schedules, pays for, consents to, and physically cooperates in the killing of her child meets the legal definition of premeditated murder, and exempting her from prosecution as perpetrator, accomplice, or conspirator is unjust nonsense.
questions.quote: **Penalties for women are prohibited**. SB2196 prohibits the prosecution of women for their involvement in killing their children even when they are doing so in defiance of this law. They cannot be prosecuted as perpetrators, accomplices, or conspirators. This is complete nonsense and utterly unjust.

questions.question: What track record does the author cite to show that 'heartbeat' and similar bills have not actually saved babies?
questions.audience: Skeptical secular reader asking why bans don't end abortion
questions.answer: She points out that every heartbeat bill, six-week ban, and near-total ban touted in pro-life headlines has been blocked in court before saving a single baby, and the Tennessee lawsuit was already drafted before passage.
questions.quote: None of the bills you have seen in pro-life headlines touted as *Heartbeat Bills*, *6 Week Bans*, *Near Total Abortion Bans*, etc. have ever gone into force—not a single one. They have all been blocked by the courts right out of the gate before one single baby was ever saved by them. In Tennessee, the lawsuit is already drawn up.

questions.question: What closing challenge does the author leave with readers who keep calling such bills steps in the right direction?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian celebrating the heartbeat bill
questions.answer: She challenges them to look at where they actually are—because if regulating, not abolishing, abortion is the result of decades of effort, the destination has already been reached.
questions.quote: So friends, when you say a heartbeat bill is *a step in the right direction*, I simply ask, what direction are you going? Please take a look around you because it is quite possible you have already arrived at your destination.

related.title: Tennessee SB 1236 Fails to Abide By its Own Preamble
related.reason: Earlier critique of a Tennessee abortion regulation bill from the same site, showing the pattern of state legislation falling short of abolition.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Provides the broader abolitionist framework Schanzenbach is applying to the Tennessee heartbeat bill.

related.title: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
related.reason: Documents the systemic failure of the pro-life political strategy that produced bills like SB2196.

related.title: Supreme Court Once Again Rejects Pro-Life Regulation
related.reason: Concrete example, published just before this article, of the courts striking down regulatory pro-life bills as Schanzenbach predicts will happen to SB2196.

related.title: June V. Russo Must Be the Death Knell of Pro-Life Judicial Supremacy and Incrementalism
related.reason: Develops the critique of submitting to SCOTUS that Schanzenbach raises in the SB2196 justice analysis.

related.title: Abolitionist Perspective on the Texas Heartbeat Bill: Three Quick Observations
related.reason: Applies the same abolitionist analysis to a later, higher-profile heartbeat bill in Texas, useful for comparison.

related.title: Former Abortion Industry Worker Exposes Ft. Worth Clinic and the Truth About Pro-Life Regulations
related.reason: Insider testimony reinforcing Schanzenbach's claim that abortionists routinely evade ultrasound and reporting requirements like those in SB2196.
